en: Is your company required to produce a Modern Slavery Statement (MSS)?
If yes, please confirm when your Modern Slavery Statement will be submitted
If your company does not have turnover equal to or greater than $100m, does your company wish to demonstrate its commitment to stopping modern slavery by producing a company statement voluntarily?
If yes, please confirm when your Modern Slavery Statement will be developed
Please confirm what elements are documented within your company statement
The statement is undersigned by a senior person within the business who can influence the enforcement of the company philosophy throughout the business
There is confirmation that where applicable, there is a formal response mechanism in the event of any reports of human trafficking or slavery
There is a reference to the prevention of underage labour being used in the manufacture and assembly of products within the supply chain
Information on the business and the supply chain including geographical locations
Commitment to the prevention of human trafficking and prevention of slavery
The statement defines the different types of slavery
The statement confirms that the content will be adopted throughout the business and all personnel employed directly or indirectly will adhere to the content
Commitment to training/guidance to the workforce on preventing human trafficking and slavery
Confirms what due diligence is undertaken to prevent human trafficking, slavery and supporting human rights throughout the supply chain
Identifies the areas of the business and the supply chain where there is a risk of slavery and human trafficking and how the company will assess and manage that risk

Confirmation that there is appropriate due diligence (This can be inhouse / independent 3rd party or combination of the two) to monitor the effectiveness of all mitigation measures and they are measured against specific performance criteria
If different or additional information is included, please specify:\n\t\n

Indicate below other company policies, management procedures or codes of conduct that have been developed and implemented throughout the business to address the potential of slavery within operations or the supply chain (Applied to all stages of the procurement and supplier evaluation process)?”
Use of a 3rd party to assist with due diligence in high risk locations
Use of a 3rd party to assist with due diligence in high risk locations
Guidance on sourcing from countries with a potential for modern day slavery
Policy / Procedure to mitigate underage labour
Guidance on sourcing from countries with a high probability of slavery
Guidance on sourcing from countries with a potential for use of underage labour
If different or additional information is included, please specify?
